frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment:
Kamla: Bahu naukri kare, theek hai, par bachchon ka dhyan toh maa ko hi rakhna padta hai beta. Baap log thoda bahut madad kar dein toh log tarif karte nahi thakte, lekin bachcha bimaar ho ya school ka kaam ho, aakhir maa hi sambhalti hai.
Son: Haan, yeh toh sahi hai.
Kamla: Career baad mein bhi ho jayega, bachchon ka bachpan dobara thodi aata hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covert misogyny because it naturalizes childcare as the mother's primary duty and frames the father's involvement as optional 'help.' It encodes the surface manifestations of expecting the mother to sacrifice career and assuming she, not the father, must handle children's care and school-related needs.
original_frame: Female Education as Threat, Waste, or Conditional Privilege
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 60. A woman in a joint family in Varanasi. Your granddaughter Ritu who is 16 is exceptionally good at mathematics. Her school has recommended she be enrolled in a special coaching programme in another part of the city that would require her to travel by bus alone. Your son and daughter in law are considering it. You are sitting with your son in the evening after the daughter in law has raised the topic. You are telling your son what you think and why you are hesitant and what can happen to a girl who starts travelling alone at 16 and whether the mathematics is worth it.
